citations.desc: Access and inspection of records and property; submission of itineraries.
citations.narrative:
An APHIS official was refused access and inspection by a facility representative. On February 3, 2026 at approximately
2:11PM, the APHIS official arrived in a parking spot in front of Andalusia Animal Adventures to conduct an unannounced
routine inspection. Upon arrival, the inspector parked in front of the facility and attempted to contact the phone number in
the account via call and text, receiving no response. While waiting for a response, a facility representative walked out of
the facility to a car in the parking lot. It was the facility representative the inspector had conducted the last routine
inspection with. The USDA official approached the facility representative and explained the purpose of the visit. The
facility representative explained that they are on winter hours and are not open to the public. The official clarified that
optimal hours were not noted in the facility’s account and, since a representative was present and able to facilitate the
inspection, the inspection needed to occur or the facility would receive a critical citation for refusal. The representative
indicated she would contact the owner and returned inside. Approximately five minutes later, the representative returned
and stated that the owner understood the seriousness of the citation but confirmed that the inspection could not be
conducted today.
Regular unannounced inspections are essential to ensure that each dealer, exhibitor, intermediate handler or carrier are
compliant with applicable rules and regulations. These inspections also allow USDA officials the opportunity to assess the
overall well-being of the animals in their care.
Each dealer, exhibitor, intermediate handler, or carrier, shall, during APHIS defined business hours, allow APHIS officials
to enter its place of business, examine and copy records required to be kept by the Act and the regulations in this part,
inspect and photograph the facilities, property and animals, as the APHIS officials consider necessary to enforce the
provisions of the Act. The licensee shall allow the inspectors to document, by the taking of photographs and other means,
conditions and areas of noncompliance. In addition, a responsible adult shall be made available to accompany APHIS
officials during the inspection process. To be corrected from this day forward, February 3, 2026.
An exit interview was refused by a facility representative.
n

narrative:
2.126(a)  Critical
   
Access and inspection of records and property; submission of itineraries. 
An APHIS official was refused access and inspection by a facility representative. On February 3, 2026 at approximately 
2:11PM, the APHIS official arrived in a parking spot in front of Andalusia Animal Adventures to conduct an unannounced 
routine inspection. Upon arrival, the inspector parked in front of the facility and attempted to contact the phone number in

facility representative explained that they are on winter hours and are not open to the public. The official clarified that 
optimal hours were not noted in the facility’s account and, since a representative was present and able to facilitate the

indicated she would contact the owner and returned inside. Approximately five minutes later, the representative returned 
and stated that the owner understood the seriousness of the citation but confirmed that the inspection could not be 
conducted today. 
 
Regular unannounced inspections are essential to ensure that each dealer, exhibitor, intermediate handler or carrier are 
compliant with applicable rules and regulations. These inspections also allow USDA officials the opportunity to assess the 
overall well-being of the animals in their care. 
 
Each dealer, exhibitor, intermediate handler, or carrier, shall, during APHIS defined business hours, allow APHIS officials 
to enter its place of business, examine and copy records required to be kept by the Act and the regulations in this part,

conditions and areas of noncompliance. In addition, a responsible adult shall be made available to accompany APHIS

An exit interview was refused by a facility representative.
